mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 330d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 330d engine typically costs £3,500–£9,500 supply only depending on generation, with the current B57D30 sitting at £5,000–£9,500. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends entirely on which generation you're asking about. The M57 (E46, early E90) is one of BMW's most durable diesels — a cast-iron block that regularly exceeds 200,000 miles. The N57 (late E90, F30) is more complex — timing chain and bottom-end bearing issues are well-documented. The B57 (G20) is BMW's modern diesel, significantly improved and more reliable. Across all, oil-change discipline is everything.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 330d?

acceptedAnswer.text: N57 timing chain wear (rear-mounted chain is the defining weak point), EGR cooler failure (recall item on N57 and B57), swirl flap failure (M57 pre-2003), bottom-end bearing wear (N57), and DPF clogging (all generations). See the Era Map above for generation-specific advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 330d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £8,000–£10,000 E90 or F30 330d, a £4,500–£5,500 recon is 45–60% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a good example. Against a £15,000+ G20, a £6,000 recon is 40% — worth it. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 330d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E46 (1999–2005) = M57D30; E90 pre-LCI (2005–2008) = M57TU2; E90 LCI (2008–2011) = N57D30; F30 (2011–2019) = N57D30; G20 (2019–present) = B57D30. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. However, the N57 chain is rear-mounted, making it an engine-out or gearbox-off job. If the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£4,500–£8,000) is usually required. At the first sign of rear-engine rattle, book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.title: The 330d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Cast-iron block legend — one of BMW's most durable diesels; swirl flaps (pre-2003) are the main weak point
Revised M57 — 231 bhp, 500 Nm; robust but EGR cooler and swirl flap issues need attention
First-gen aluminium-block N57 — timing chain at rear; more complex than M57, needs stricter maintenance
Mature N57 — revised timing chain guides (2015+); ZF8 gearbox is the star; chain and bottom-end bearings need monitoring
Modern B57 straight-six — significantly improved chain durability; valve cover and oil filter housing leaks are the main watchpoints

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current G20/B57 generation, the most common quote request today. Older generations: see the Era Map above for E46/M57, E90/N57 and F30/N57-specific pricing, which can vary significantly.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 330d engine typically takes 10–16 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). N57 timing chain replacement is an engine-out or gearbox-off job, adding significant labour. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare 330d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N57 Timing Chain Tensioner & Guide Wear (E90 LCI, F30)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the rear of the engine on cold start, metallic grinding at 2,000 rpm, engine warning light, loss of power.
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (timing chain, tensioner and guide replacement — engine-out or gearbox-off job)
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,000 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The N57's rear-mounted timing chain is the defining weak point of this engine generation. Unlike the front-mounted M57 chain, N57 chain replacement is a labour-intensive job. From 2015 onwards, BMW revised the guides with filled-in ribs to reduce failure rates, but the risk remains. Regular oil changes (10,000 miles max, not BMW's CBS intervals) are the best prevention. At the first sign of rear-engine rattle, get it inspected immediately — a £1,500–£3,500 preventative chain replacement is dramatically cheaper than a £4,500+ engine swap.

problems.title: Swirl Flap Failure (M57 — E46, Early E90)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, rough idle, engine warning light, occasional rattling from the intake manifold, black smoke.
problems.replacementCost: £1,000–£2,500 (intake manifold replacement, or engine repair if debris entered a cylinder)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.recommendation: The M57's swirl flaps in the intake manifold can fail, with the metal flap breaking off and being ingested into the engine — catastrophic if it happens. This affects pre-2003 M57 engines most severely, but post-2003 revisions reduced the risk. Preventative blanking plates (£100–£300) remove the risk entirely with no noticeable performance loss. If your E46 or early E90 330d hasn't had this done, consider it essential preventative maintenance.

problems.title: EGR Cooler Failure (N57 & B57)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, occasional burning smell, engine warning light.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — subject to BMW recalls
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless coolant loss leads to overheating damage)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: check recall status immediately; can cause fire risk

problems.recommendation: EGR cooler failure is a well-documented issue across N57 and B57 engines. BMW issued multiple recalls for this — the latest with an updated part number. A leaking EGR cooler can allow coolant into the exhaust system, and in some cases, can create a fire risk. Check your vehicle's recall status immediately — if your 330d hasn't had the updated EGR cooler fitted, get it done. This is a safety-critical item.

problems.title: N57 Bottom-End Bearing Failure
problems.symptoms: Knocking noise from the bottom of the engine, especially under load, low oil pressure warning, eventual engine seizure.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear knocking, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: N57 engines can suffer from bottom-end bearing failure, particularly on higher-mileage examples. The twin-turbo versions (335d) seem to suffer more than the single-turbo 330d, but it's still a known risk. Regular oil changes with the correct BMW-spec oil are the key prevention — many specialists recommend 8,000–10,000 mile intervals rather than BMW's CBS schedule. If you hear a knocking noise from the bottom end, stop driving immediately and have it inspected.

problems.title: DPF & Emissions System Issues (All Generations)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced power, increased fuel consumption, frequent regeneration cycles, MOT emissions failure.
problems.repairCost: £200–£800 (DPF clean, forced regeneration, or sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can lead to costly emissions system damage

problems.recommendation: All modern diesels — M57, N57 and B57 — suffer from DPF issues if used primarily for short urban journeys. The DPF needs regular motorway runs to regenerate passively. If the car has been used mostly for short trips, the DPF can clog, leading to expensive repairs. Regular motorway driving and using premium diesel (e.g., Shell V-Power) can help keep the DPF healthy.

Viable if caught early; often requires full rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 330d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 10,000-mile intervals (not CBS extended schedule)
* EGR cooler recall completed (check with BMW)
* N57 cars (2011–2019): no cold-start rattle from the rear of the engine
* M57 cars (pre-2011): swirl flaps blanked or replaced
* B57 cars (2019+): clean service history and no coolant loss
avoidIf: * N57 with cold-start rattle — timing chain work is imminent and expensive
* No proof of regular oil changes — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* EGR cooler not replaced — potential fire risk
* Any knocking from the bottom end — bearing failure is likely
* DPF warnings with no diagnosis — emissions system repairs can be costly

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW 330d represents one of the finest diesel engines ever made. The M57 is a cast-iron legend; the N57 offers more power but demands stricter maintenance; the B57 is BMW's modern masterpiece. Across all three generations, one rule applies: oil change discipline is everything. BMW's extended CBS servicing is widely considered inadequate — treat your 330d to 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes and it will reward you with hundreds of thousands of miles of effortless motoring. Buy on history, not on low mileage alone.

cta.label: → Compare 330d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 330d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle from the rear of the engine (N57 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (M57/N57/B57) and confirmed oil-change discipline (10k miles max)
* EGR Cooler — Check recall status; this is a safety-critical item on N57 and B57
* Swirl Flaps — M57 cars: have they been blanked? (Pre-2003 cars are the highest risk)
* DPF Health — Ask about regeneration frequency; urban cars will have more issues
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles? (N57 oil consumption is a known issue)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, EGR, DPF, or emissions codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (EGR, DPF, NOx sensor issues)

items.label: SECTION 8 — FAQ
items.value: 1. How much does a BMW 330d engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.label: A reconditioned 330d engine typically costs £3,500–£9,500 supply only depending on generation, with the current B57D30 sitting at £5,000–£9,500. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends entirely on which generation you're asking about. The M57 (E46, early E90) is one of BMW's most durable diesels — a cast-iron block that regularly exceeds 200,000 miles. The N57 (late E90, F30) is more complex — timing chain and bottom-end bearing issues are well-documented. The B57 (G20) is BMW's modern diesel, significantly improved and more reliable. Across all, oil-change discipline is everything.
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the BMW 330d?

items.label: N57 timing chain wear (rear-mounted chain is the defining weak point), EGR cooler failure (recall item on N57 and B57), swirl flap failure (M57 pre-2003), bottom-end bearing wear (N57), and DPF clogging (all generations). See the Era Map above for generation-specific advice.
items.value: 4. Is it worth replacing a BMW 330d engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.label: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £8,000–£10,000 E90 or F30 330d, a £4,500–£5,500 recon is 45–60% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a good example. Against a £15,000+ G20, a £6,000 recon is 40% — worth it. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.
items.value: 5. How do I find my BMW 330d engine code?

items.label: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E46 (1999–2005) = M57D30; E90 pre-LCI (2005–2008) = M57TU2; E90 LCI (2008–2011) = N57D30; F30 (2011–2019) = N57D30; G20 (2019–present) = B57D30. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.
items.value: 6. Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

items.label: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500. However, the N57 chain is rear-mounted, making it an engine-out or gearbox-off job. If the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£4,500–£8,000) is usually required. At the first sign of rear-engine rattle, book an inspection immediately.
items.value: Disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes.
